about: Fixtures for the format adapters (docs/specs/format-ingestion-mapping.md, F-items).
Unlike expected/hocon/, these expectations are NOT generated from Lightbend.
Lightbend has no equivalent of these adapters, so there is no oracle: the
expectations encode the project's own F-item decisions, and the differential
value is that the four implementations agree with them and with each other.
They live under testdata/ rather than expected/ for that reason, and because
YAML fixtures deliberately quote any scalar whose resolution differs between
YAML 1.1 and 1.2 (010, no, 1:30 and friends). Scalar resolution belongs to
the YAML library in use, not to this spec, so asserting on those forms would
test the library rather than the adapter. See F5 'Scope'.

cases.note: F1.1/F1.2/F1.3 prefix filter, __ separator, lowercased segments
F1.6 two names reach one path and the environment has no order to break the tie
F1.7 the small dialect; ${x} inside single quotes stays literal (F0.2)
F1.7 a trailing # is refused rather than guessed at
F3.2 line and block comments, trailing commas
comment markers inside string literals are data; F0.2 ${...} stays literal
F0.3 a config root must be an object
F4.2 HOCON has no datetime, so all four TOML date-time types are strings
F4.4 arrays of tables become arrays of objects; F0.2 ${...} stays literal
F0.6 infinity is not representable in HOCON
F5.2 aliases and merge keys resolve before mapping; an explicit key wins over a merged one, and no << may survive

F5.3 non-string scalar keys stringify. The integer and boolean keys sit in separate mappings on purpose: Python's bool subclasses int, so 1 and true collide as dict keys and a document with both as siblings is not representable there — a language-natural divergence, like S1.2.6. The quoted 010 and no are deliberate; see 'about'.
F5.7 decoding the first document and dropping the rest would be silent data loss
F5.9 an empty document is the empty object, as an empty HOCON document is (S3.1)
F5.5 HOCON has no binary type, so !!binary keeps its base64 text
F0.6 NaN is not representable in HOCON
